title: The Third Sunday of Advent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
December 12, 2021 | Third Sunday of Advent | Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Third Sunday of Advent, traditionally known as "Gaudete Sunday" (from the Latin for "rejoice"), falls during the heart of the Advent season preparing for Christmas. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would likely include Isaiah 61:1-4, 8-11 (the spirit of the Lord upon me); Psalm 126; 1 Thessalonians 5:16-25; and John 1:6-8, 19-28 (John the Baptist's testimony). The season emphasizes joyful anticipation and the coming of Christ, with particular focus on John the Baptist as the forerunner preparing the way.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in late 2021 was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention (held virtually in July 2021) and navigating post-pandemic reopening protocols in dioceses nationwide. The Diocese of Colorado, led by Bishop Kimberly Lucas (who had been elected in 2018), was managing the continued recovery of parish life after COVID-19 lockdowns; Saint John's Cathedral itself had been operating under modified capacity and hybrid worship models throughout 2021. No major denominational crises or bishop elections were occurring at this specific moment, though tensions over liturgical change and COVID safety protocols remained present in many parishes nationally.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing early December winter weather typical for the season, with temperatures in the 30s-40s Fahrenheit and potential for snow as Christmas approached. The city was focused on holiday season preparations and recovery from the Delta variant surge that had affected Colorado hospitals in late August and September. No major local political or community crises were dominating local news in the specific week before December 12, though the city remained engaged in broader discussions about homelessness and housing that characterized Denver's 2021 civic discourse.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Build Back Better Act was in critical legislative negotiations in Congress, with Senator Joe Manchin announcing his opposition on December 19 (one week after this sermon), creating significant uncertainty about the Biden administration's domestic agenda going forward. The nation was experiencing rising inflation (reaching 6.8% year-over-year in November 2021, the highest in 39 years) and supply chain disruptions affecting holiday shopping and goods availability. The Omicron COVID-19 variant was beginning to spread in the U.S. (first confirmed case December 1), causing growing concern about another winter surge even as vaccination campaigns continued and many Americans pursued holiday travel plans.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The world was watching the ongoing Russian military buildup on the Ukrainian border, with intelligence reports warning of potential invasion plans—a crisis that would escalate dramatically after this date but was already a significant diplomatic concern in December 2021. The Astroworld Festival tragedy in Houston (November 5) and its ongoing legal and cultural aftermath remained a topic of national discussion regarding concert safety and crowd control. The COP26 climate conference had concluded in Glasgow in early November with mixed results on emissions commitments, leaving environmental concerns at the forefront of international discourse heading into the Christmas season.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America in mid-December 2021 occupied an uneasy cultural space: Christmas commercialism was in full swing with holiday shopping and entertainment, yet underlying anxiety about COVID variants, economic inflation, and political division created a somewhat subdued national mood compared to pre-pandemic holiday seasons. Entertainment focus included the release of Spider-Man: No Way Home (December 17, just after this date), which was generating significant cultural conversation about the Marvel multiverse. There was a general sense of cautious normalcy—people were eager to return to in-person celebrations and gatherings after 2020's isolation, but hesitancy and concern about health and safety lingered, making the Advent themes of hopeful waiting and joyful anticipation particularly resonant for congregational worship.
